rootCause: The live chat branch in `remote-release-smoke.spec.ts` asserted an exact `CHAT_PROMPT` text match immediately after Send. In production, the message renderer can normalize markdown and line wrapping so exact-text lookup is not a durable DOM contract, which produced a false-negative even when user send/assistant receive succeeded.
resolution: Replaced the brittle prompt-text assertion with a durable message-flow contract: assert the user message count increments after Send, then wait for assistant reply vs. error banner and require assistant success with no error banner visible.
